title: Filix-mas squamigera
intro: Filix-mas squamigera, also known as the lobe-leaved lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Filix-mas squamigera include sesquiterpenoids, triterpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Filix-mas squamigera is a species of fern. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Filix-mas is a subgenus within the genus Dryopteris. The classification of Filix-mas squamigera is further specified as Dryopteris squamigera. It is also known as the hairy buckler fern or the hairy wood fern.
uses: Filix-mas squamigera, also known as the hairy fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's fronds have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, the hairy fern is used to balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Filix-mas squamigera.

parts: Filix-mas squamigera, also known as the hairy fern or the scaly male fern, is primarily used for its medicinal properties. The plant's fronds are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. They are believed to be effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions and wounds. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat fever and rheumatism. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its saponin content, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects.
preparations: Filix-mas squamigera, commonly known as the scaly male fern, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. side_effects: Filix-mas squamigera, commonly known as the rough fern, is not typically used for medicinal purposes.
